source_pr.title: executor: open childExec during execution for UnionExec
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #21441 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

re-revert https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/21561

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

Proposal: [xxx](url) <!-- REMOVE this line if not applicable -->

What's Changed:
Before this commit, `Union` opens all the child executors during the `Open` phase.
This increases the overhead of `buildRequest` and the risk of OOM.

After this commit, the `Open` of childExec in `Union` is moved to the `Next` phase. The childExec be opened simultaneously is limited to `tidb_executor_concurrency` atmost.

How it Works:

### Related changes

N/A

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Integration test
- Manual test
Suppose we have a table `t`, it contains 100 partitions, each partition contains 10,000,000 tuples.
The result of `select * from t;`
Before:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/9039012/119595037-7c0dd400-be0f-11eb-82bb-106ca8cdf229.png)
After:
The query works fine.

Side effects

- The speed of UnionExec may decrease if the child of Union is larger than `tidb_executor_concurrency`.

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- Refine the execution mechanism `UnionExec` to reduce the overhead of `UnionExec`.

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("select a from (" + sql + ") t order by a limit 4").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "1", "1", "1")) (executor/executor_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("select a from (" + sql + ") t order by a limit 4").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "1", "1", "1")) (executor/executor_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: tk.MustQuery("select a from (" + sql + ") t order by a limit 4").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "1", "1", "1")) (executor/executor_test.go).
fix_pattern: tk.MustQuery("select a from (" + sql + ") t order by a limit 4").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "1", "1", "1")) (executor/executor_test.go)

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select a from (" + sql + ") t order by a limit 4").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "1", "1", "1")) (executor/executor_test.go)
